vocabulary.definition: to laugh in a loud, harsh way
vocabulary.example: Over and over again he dreamed about his parents disappearing in a flash of green light, while a high voice cackled with laughter.

vocabulary.definition: giving the feeling that something harmful or evil is happening
vocabulary.example: Speaking quietly so that no one else would hear, Harry told the other two about Snape’s sudden, sinister desire to be a Quidditch referee.

vocabulary.definition: a magic spell meant to cause harm
vocabulary.example: his legs had been stuck together with what they recognized at once as the Leg Locker Curse.

vocabulary.definition: to speak quickly and unclearly, often because you are shocked or eating
vocabulary.example: he spluttered through a mouthful of mud.

vocabulary.definition: an ancient study of turning metals into gold and seeking magical substances
vocabulary.example: and his work on alchemy with his partner, Nicolas Flamel’!

vocabulary.definition: able to live forever and never die
vocabulary.example: It also produces the Elixir of Life, which will make the drinker immortal.

vocabulary.definition: the official who makes sure players follow the rules in a game
vocabulary.example: Snape’s refereeing this time, and he’ll be looking for any excuse to knock points off Gryffindor!

vocabulary.definition: a movement where you turn your body over in the air or head over heels

vocabulary.example: Harry had pulled out of the dive, his arm raised in triumph, the Snitch clasped in his hand.

vocabulary.definition: to move quietly as if hunting or sneaking
vocabulary.example: He recognized the figure’s prowling walk.

vocabulary.definition: so frightened that you cannot move
vocabulary.example: Harry could see Quirrell, standing quite still as though he was petrified.

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: Still haunted by the Mirror of Erised while trying to learn who Nicolas Flamel is.

characters.name: Hermione Granger
characters.description: Returns from holiday and helps crack the mystery of Flamel and the Stone.

characters.name: Ron Weasley
characters.description: Supports Harry through nightmares, the Quidditch match, and the forest spy mission.

characters.name: Severus Snape
characters.description: Referees the Quidditch match and is later seen threatening Quirrell in the forest.

background.hook: Harry's nightmares won't stop, and the trio still cannot find Nicolas Flamel in the library. Then a Chocolate Frog card changes everything — just before a Quidditch match refereed by Snape leads Harry to spy on a secret forest meeting.
background.readingFocus: Watch how the trio finally learns what the Stone is. Notice what Harry overhears between Snape and Quirrell, and how that changes what they think Snape wants.

options.text: a very frightening dream
a type of school exam
a Quidditch position
making gold and magical substances like the Sorcerer's Stone
flying on broomsticks
training dragons for pets
can live forever and never die
can turn invisible at will
are afraid of the dark

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: The trio discovers Nicolas Flamel made the Sorcerer's Stone, Harry wins a Quidditch match, and he spies on Snape and Quirrell in the forest
Harry finds the Mirror of Erised again and sees his parents
Hagrid hatches a dragon egg in his hut
The trio sits detention in the Forbidden Forest

comprehension.explanation: Harry learns Flamel's identity and what the Stone does, wins the match against Hufflepuff, then follows Snape into the forest and overhears him pressuring Quirrell about the Stone.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry finally learn who Nicolas Flamel is?

options.text: He reads about him on the back of a Chocolate Frog card
Hagrid tells him during tea in the hut
Snape mentions him in Potions class
He finds Flamel's name in a library index

comprehension.explanation: Neville gives Harry a Dumbledore Famous Wizard card whose back mentions Flamel and alchemy.

comprehension.stem: What are two powers of the Sorcerer's Stone?

options.text: It turns any metal into pure gold and produces the Elixir of Life
It grants invisibility and cures werewolf bites
It summons dragons and unlocks any door
It writes homework answers and fixes broken brooms

comprehension.explanation: Hermione reads that the Stone transforms metal into gold and makes the Elixir of Life.

comprehension.stem: Why is the Gryffindor team worried about Snape refereeing?

options.text: They think he will look for excuses to take points from Gryffindor
Snape has never seen a Quidditch match before
Snape plans to play Seeker for Slytherin
Dumbledore banned Snape from the pitch

comprehension.explanation: Wood warns that Snape will favor Hufflepuff and knock points off Gryffindor.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry feel when he sees Dumbledore watching the match?

options.text: Relieved — he feels safe because Snape would not dare hurt him with Dumbledore there
Angry — he wanted to quit the team
Embarrassed — he forgot how to fly
Jealous — he wishes Snape were playing instead

comprehension.explanation: Harry's heart does a somersault with relief; he thinks Snape cannot try to hurt him while Dumbledore watches.

comprehension.stem: What does Harry conclude after overhearing Snape and Quirrell in the forest?

options.text: Snape is trying to force Quirrell to help him steal the Stone and get past Fluffy
Quirrell is helping Snape study for exams
Snape and Quirrell are planning a surprise party for Hagrid
The Stone has already been moved to London

comprehension.explanation: Harry tells Ron and Hermione that Snape asked Quirrell how to get past Fluffy and mentioned his 'hocus pocus.'

comprehension.stem: How quickly does Harry catch the Snitch in the match?

options.text: In about five minutes — faster than anyone can remember
After three full hours of play
He never catches it; Hufflepuff wins
The match is canceled because of rain

comprehension.explanation: The game barely lasted five minutes and no one could remember the Snitch being caught so quickly.

options.text: a very frightening dream
a type of homework
a house-elf servant
a Quidditch foul

vocabulary.explanation: Harry keeps dreaming about green light and a laughing voice after seeing the mirror.

vocabulary.stem: Harry told the other two about Snape's sudden, ___ desire to be a Quidditch referee.

options.text: sinister
famous
miserable
normal

vocabulary.explanation: Harry quietly warns Ron and Hermione that Snape's refereeing plan feels threatening.

options.text: lives forever and does not die
turns metal into gold
guards a trapdoor
referees a Quidditch match

vocabulary.explanation: The Elixir of Life from the Stone would make the drinker immortal.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'triumph'?

options.text: victory
punishment
whisper
nightmare

vocabulary.explanation: Harry raises his arm in triumph after catching the Snitch.

vocabulary.stem: Quirrell stood quite still as though he was ___.

options.text: petrified
immortal
sinister
famous

vocabulary.explanation: After Snape leaves the clearing, Quirrell stays frozen in fear.

cloze.title: Nicolas Flamel
paragraphs: Harry cannot forget the mirror and starts having (1) ___. After Neville is hit with the Leg Locker (2) ___, a Chocolate Frog card leads them to Nicolas Flamel and the study of (3) ___.
They learn the Sorcerer's Stone makes gold and an elixir that would make the drinker (4) ___. Snape is (5) ___ the Quidditch match, but Harry catches the Snitch in (6) ___ anyway.
That night Harry follows Snape into the forest, recognizes his (7) ___ walk, and overhears him threaten Quirrell about the Stone.

gaps.explanation: Harry starts having nightmares about green light and laughter.
Malfoy used the Leg Locker Curse on Neville.
Flamel's work on alchemy with Dumbledore is mentioned on the card.
The Elixir of Life would make the drinker immortal.
Wood says Snape is refereeing and will look for excuses against Gryffindor.
Harry pulls out of the dive with his arm raised in triumph.
Harry recognizes Snape by his prowling walk toward the forest.
